_metadata.description: Flowstarter agent tools specification. These tools describe the actual capabilities available in the application. Tools are invoked via XML action tags in LLM responses, not standard function calling.

file.description: Create or write files to the project. Overwrites existing files completely. Use for creating new files or complete rewrites. For modifying existing files, prefer using the 'line-replace' action if possible.

content.description: Complete file content - code, configuration, CSS, or any text

line-replace.description: Modify specific lines in existing files using exact line numbers. Preferred method for editing existing code. Maintains file integrity and allows precise edits without rewriting entire files. Works with any language or file type.
line-replace.invocation: <flowstarterAction type="line-replace" filePath="path/to/file" firstLine="15" lastLine="28" search="exact content to find" replace="new content"></flowstarterAction>

search.description: Exact content to search for. Use '...' for omitted sections in large blocks.

examples.code: <flowstarterAction type="line-replace" filePath="src/utils.ts" firstLine="5" lastLine="8" search="return a + b;" replace="return a * b;"></flowstarterAction>

shell.description: Execute shell commands in the Daytona sandbox environment. Has access to pnpm, npm, bun, and other CLI tools. Use for installing dependencies, running builds, etc.

command.description: Shell command to execute (e.g., 'pnpm add package', 'npm run build', 'git commit -m message')

supabase.description: Perform database operations: create migrations, execute SQL queries, or manage database schema. Supports PostgreSQL SQL syntax for Supabase backend.

operations.migration: Create a new database migration file

operation.description: Operation type: 'migration' to create a migration file, 'query' to execute SQL

content.example: CREATE TABLE users (id uuid PRIMARY KEY, name text);

examples.code:
<flowstarterAction type="supabase" operation="migration">
CREATE TABLE users (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name text NOT NULL,
  email text UNIQUE,
  created_at timestamp DEFAULT now()
);
</flowstarterAction>

<flowstarterAction type="supabase" operation="migration">
CREATE TABLE auth_users (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY,
  email text UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  password_hash text,
  created_at timestamp DEFAULT now()
);
</flowstarterAction>

mcp-tools.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools provide extensibility through external MCP servers. Available tools depend on configured MCP servers. Common available tools include Supabase database, Stripe payments, GitHub operations, and more.
mcp-tools.configuration: Configured through the MCP settings panel in the application

available_servers.name: Supabase
available_servers.tools: Database queries, table management, RLS policies

available_servers.name: Claude Code
available_servers.tools: Code search, file operations, terminal access

mcp-tools.invocation: MCP tools are invoked directly by name (after LLM function calling capability is enabled)
mcp-tools.note: These tools are optional and must be explicitly configured by the user. Not all users have MCP servers available.
